title: Lesson Overview
content: Students explore familiar places where AI may appear then explain their thinking as a class.
content_json: {"teacher_note":"Keep examples concrete and local to school life."}

title: Opening Question
content: Where have you seen technology help someone today?
content_json: {"teacher_note":"Accept school, home, shop, translation, and transport examples."}

title: Teacher-Led Flow
content: Guide students through naming familiar technology, sorting examples, and explaining their reasoning.
content_json: {"steps":["Name familiar technology","Sort examples as a class","Invite reasoning after each choice","Close with one careful-use reminder"]}

title: Discussion Prompt
content: Which of these tools might learn from examples or patterns?

title: Assessment Guidance
content: Look for whether students can name one possible AI example and explain their thinking in simple language.
content_json: {"look_for":["Names one AI example","Explains using patterns or examples","Listens to peer reasoning"]}

title: Misconceptions and Pitfalls
content: Avoid implying every computer tool is AI. Help students explain uncertainty with might or maybe.
content_json: {"watch_for":["Students call every device AI","Students focus only on robots"]}

title: Family Connection
content: Students may ask at home: What technology helps our family, and how do we use it carefully?
content_json: {"send_home_note":"Optional oral family discussion; no accounts or personal data required."}
